title: Automotive 5V Rail (9–16V Input)
description: A wide-input buck converter for automotive 12 V systems, tolerating load-dump transients (up to 36 V) and cold-crank sag (down to 9 V) while delivering 5 V at 3 A to body-electronics MCUs.
design_notes: The 9–16 V input range covers the full automotive 12 V battery operating window including cold-start cranking (9 V) and alternator peak voltage (14.4 V nominal, 16 V worst-case). Load-dump transients can reach 36 V for 400 ms under ISO 7637 pulse 5a — this design requires either a TVS clamp on the input rail or a controller with built-in over-voltage protection. The 70 °C ambient temperature is a conservative worst-case for an underbonnet ECU; derate the MOSFET's Rds(on) accordingly (typically +80 % from 25 °C to 125 °C junction). AEC-Q100 Grade 1 rated components should be specified for production. The 400 kHz switching frequency keeps the inductor below 10 µH while remaining within the lower switching-frequency preference of automotive EMI standards (CISPR 25).
source: TI SLVA704 — Automotive Buck Converter Design Guide; CISPR 25 Ed.4
